questions.q: Bipin Chandra Pal was one member of which famous nationalist trio?
questions.explanation: He formed the assertive 'Lal-Bal-Pal' trio with Lala Lajpat Rai and Bal Gangadhar Tilak.

questions.q: Which province did Bipin Chandra Pal primarily belong to?
questions.explanation: Bipin Chandra Pal was a Bengali nationalist leader, the 'Pal' of the Lal-Bal-Pal trio.

questions.q: Bipin Chandra Pal belonged to which wing of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: He was a leading figure of the assertive 'Extremist' wing that pressed for bolder methods than the Moderates.

questions.q: Which movement, sparked by the 1905 Partition of Bengal, did Bipin Chandra Pal champion?
questions.explanation: He was one of the foremost champions of the Swadeshi movement during the agitation against the Partition of Bengal.

questions.q: Bipin Chandra Pal was renowned for being a powerful what?
questions.explanation: He was celebrated as a fiery orator and a prolific journalist during the Swadeshi era.

questions.q: Along with Swadeshi, which related method did Bipin Chandra Pal advocate against British goods?
questions.explanation: He promoted boycott of British goods alongside Swadeshi as forms of political resistance.

questions.q: Which cause concerning Indian schooling did Bipin Chandra Pal support?
questions.explanation: He championed national education as an alternative to colonial-controlled schooling during the Swadeshi movement.

questions.q: The Partition of Bengal that fuelled Bipin Chandra Pal's activism occurred in which year?
questions.explanation: Lord Curzon's Partition of Bengal in 1905 triggered the Swadeshi movement that Pal led in Bengal.

questions.q: Bipin Chandra Pal is often remembered with the title 'Father of' which type of movement in India?
questions.explanation: He is frequently called a father of revolutionary and assertive nationalist thought in India.

questions.q: The 'Bal' in the Lal-Bal-Pal trio that included Bipin Chandra Pal referred to whom?
questions.explanation: The trio consisted of Lala Lajpat Rai (Lal), Bal Gangadhar Tilak (Bal), and Bipin Chandra Pal (Pal).

questions.q: In which year was Bipin Chandra Pal born?
questions.explanation: Bipin Chandra Pal was born on 7 November 1858 in Sylhet, in present-day Bangladesh.

questions.q: In which year did Bipin Chandra Pal die?
questions.explanation: He died on 20 May 1932 in Calcutta.

questions.q: Which fiery nationalist newspaper did Bipin Chandra Pal launch in 1906?
questions.explanation: He founded the English daily 'Bande Mataram' in 1906, with Aurobindo Ghosh closely associated with it.

questions.q: Bipin Chandra Pal refused to testify in the sedition case against which fellow nationalist, leading to his own imprisonment?
questions.explanation: In the Bande Mataram sedition case he refused to give evidence against Aurobindo Ghosh and was jailed for six months.

questions.q: In his early life, Bipin Chandra Pal was associated with which religious reform movement?
questions.explanation: He joined the Brahmo Samaj early in life and was influenced by its reformist ideas.

questions.q: Bipin Chandra Pal began his weekly journal 'Paridarshak' in which province?
questions.explanation: He started the Bengali weekly 'Paridarshak' in 1880 in Sylhet, then part of Assam.

questions.q: Which slogan or programme did Bipin Chandra Pal promote, combining Swadeshi, boycott, national education and passive resistance?
options: The fourfold programme of the Extremists
questions.explanation: Pal advanced the Extremists' fourfold programme of Swadeshi, boycott, national education, and passive resistance.

questions.q: Bipin Chandra Pal spent time lecturing on India's cause in which country in 1908-1911?
questions.explanation: He moved to England around 1908 and continued nationalist propaganda and writing there for a few years.

questions.q: In which present-day country was Bipin Chandra Pal's birthplace of Sylhet located?
questions.explanation: Sylhet, his birthplace, lies in present-day Bangladesh; it was part of British Bengal/Assam at the time.

questions.q: Later in his career, Bipin Chandra Pal grew critical of which leader's methods and political dominance?
questions.explanation: In his later years he became a vocal critic of Gandhi's leadership and certain non-cooperation methods.

questions.q: Bipin Chandra Pal's oratory was especially influential in spreading Swadeshi ideas in which region during 1907?
questions.explanation: His 1907 lecture tour through Madras and the south greatly energized the Swadeshi movement in that region.

questions.q: Which famous Bengali revolutionary and philosopher worked closely with Bipin Chandra Pal on 'Bande Mataram'?
questions.explanation: Aurobindo Ghosh was a close collaborator on the 'Bande Mataram' newspaper that Pal founded.

questions.q: On what date in 1858 was Bipin Chandra Pal born?
questions.explanation: He was born on 7 November 1858 in Poil village, Sylhet district.

questions.q: Bipin Chandra Pal served briefly as a librarian at which institution early in his career?
questions.explanation: He worked for a time as a librarian at the Calcutta Public Library before fully entering journalism and politics.

questions.q: What was the name of the monthly journal Bipin Chandra Pal edited that promoted his political philosophy?
questions.explanation: He edited and published the journal 'New India' to disseminate his nationalist and political views.

questions.q: Bipin Chandra Pal advocated which constitutional ideal as the goal of Indian self-rule, distinct from full separation?
options: Self-government within a federation of free nations / imperial federation
questions.explanation: Pal articulated ideas of swaraj framed as self-government within a federation of free and equal nations.

questions.q: For how long was Bipin Chandra Pal imprisoned in connection with the Bande Mataram sedition case?
questions.explanation: He was sentenced to six months' imprisonment for refusing to testify in the Bande Mataram case in 1907-08.

questions.q: Bipin Chandra Pal worked alongside which leader to bring the Surat Congress disputes to a head in 1907?
questions.explanation: As an Extremist leader, Pal aligned with Tilak in the confrontation with Moderates that caused the 1907 Surat Split.

questions.q: Which autobiographical work did Bipin Chandra Pal write describing his life and times?
options: Memories of My Life and Times
The Story of My Experiments with Truth
questions.explanation: He authored 'Memories of My Life and Times,' an account of his personal and political journey.

questions.q: In which city did Bipin Chandra Pal die in 1932?
questions.explanation: He died on 20 May 1932 in Calcutta, somewhat estranged from mainstream Congress politics in his final years.
